title: JavaScript Functions Knowledge Check
description: Check your understanding of function declarations, expressions, parameters, return values, default parameters, rest parameters, and IIFEs.
author: DailyCoder

questions.prompt: What is the main purpose of a function?

options.text: To wrap reusable logic that can be called when needed
To automatically convert strings into numbers
To stop JavaScript from using objects

questions.explanation: A function lets you package reusable logic and call it whenever that behavior is needed.

questions.prompt: Which syntax is a function declaration?
questions.explanation: A function declaration starts with the `function` keyword followed by the function name.

options.text: It throws because declarations cannot be hoisted

questions.explanation: Function declarations are hoisted, so the function can be called before its definition appears in the code.

options.text: It fails because `sayHello` is used before initialization

questions.explanation: A function expression assigned to `const` is not usable before the assignment line runs.
`a` and `b` are parameters because they are named placeholders in the function definition.
The function returns the result of `a + b`, so `add(2, 3)` returns `5`.

questions.prompt: What does a function return if it has no `return` statement?
questions.explanation: JavaScript functions return `undefined` by default when no value is explicitly returned.

options.text: It logs a value and keeps the function running
It sends a value back to the caller and stops the function
It converts a function declaration into an expression

questions.explanation: `return` sends a value back to where the function was called and immediately exits the function.

questions.prompt: When is a default parameter used?

options.text: When the argument is omitted or `undefined`

questions.explanation: Default parameters apply only when the matching argument is missing or explicitly `undefined`.
`null` is a real value, so it does not trigger the default parameter.

questions.prompt: What does a rest parameter do?

options.text: It stops a function before it returns
It gathers remaining arguments into a real array
It makes all parameters optional by default
It spreads an object into global variables

questions.explanation: A rest parameter uses `...name` to collect remaining arguments into an array.

questions.prompt: Which function uses a rest parameter correctly?
questions.explanation: A rest parameter uses `...` before the name, and it must be the final parameter.
The first argument goes into `first`; the remaining arguments are gathered into the `rest` array.

options.text: A function expression that runs immediately after it is created
A function that can only be called after a timeout
A rest parameter with no arguments

questions.explanation: An IIFE is an Immediately Invoked Function Expression, commonly written as `(function () { ... })();`.

questions.prompt: What is the classic reason for using an IIFE?

options.text: To create a private scope and avoid leaking variables globally
To force all arguments to become strings

questions.explanation: IIFEs were commonly used to create private scope, especially before `let`, `const`, and ES modules were common.
